misconceptionSummary: Students assume a sequence is linear after checking too little evidence or mix up difference with ratio.

lines.text: Claim: linear because it keeps going up

steps.narration: The differences are not constant, so the sequence is not linear.
The pattern is multiplicative, not additive.

lines.text: Linear means add or subtract the same amount each time

steps.narration: That is what makes a sequence linear.

visuals.expression: Check differences, not just whether the numbers rise
visuals.label: Linear needs constant difference

steps.narration: To avoid the mistake, test the differences carefully.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: confusing a doubling pattern with a linear pattern. Linear sequences depend on constant difference, not constant multiplier.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: If the difference changes, the sequence is not linear, even if it keeps increasing.
